Week 4 | Knowledge Check: Evaluating Thesis Statements

Evaluating Thesis Statements
Instructions
Read each thesis statement and determine if they are strong or weak. Flip the card to check your work!



After you finish this page, click Next to continue to Specifics of Grammar.


Week 4 | Specifics of Grammar

Specifics of Grammar
We will visit three grammar topics before we end our lessons: misplaced modifiers, dangling modifiers, and run-on sentences.

A misplaced modifier is a word, phrase, or clause that needs to be in the right place in relation to the word it describes. It is incorrectly positioned in relation to the word it describes or modifies. The result is a sentence that lacks coherence, feels clumsy, or conveys unintended meanings.

An example might be: Running down the street, the dog chased the ball.
